summary: PR #59695 remains the canonical repair path for replacing the remaining SHA-1 configuration/media test fingerprints with SHA-256. It is open, narrow, maintainer-editable, and should be repaired on the contributor branch; merge/close/comment actions are blocked by job policy.

actions.reason: Repair the existing contributor branch instead of replacing it: the PR is useful, focused, and maintainer-editable, but it still needs fresh branch repair/validation before any future merge decision.

Build an executable contributor-branch repair artifact so the executor can rebase/update #59695, preserve YonganZhang's credit, run focused proof plus pnpm check:changed, and leave merge decisions to a later allowed lane.

repair_candidate.summary: Repair PR #59695 on the existing contributor branch by keeping the SHA-256 fingerprinting change, preserving the no-persisted-migration runtime comment, and validating the runtime fingerprint test plus media test surface against current main.
repair_candidate.pr_title: Replace SHA-1 with SHA-256 for config fingerprinting
repair_candidate.pr_body:
## Summary
- Keep @YonganZhang's SHA-1 to SHA-256 cleanup for session MCP config fingerprints and media-understanding test fixture cache hashes.
- Keep the runtime comment documenting that session MCP fingerprints only invalidate in-memory runtime catalogs, so the 40 to 64 character hash change causes at most one cache miss and does not need a persisted migration.
- Keep focused coverage that config fingerprints are 64-character hex strings and differ when the MCP config changes.

## Credit
Source PR: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/59695 by @YonganZhang. This repair preserves the contributor branch path and attribution.

## Verification
- node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/agent-bundle-mcp-runtime.test.ts -t "recreates the session runtime when MCP config changes"
- node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/media-understanding/apply.test.ts
- pnpm check:changed

## Notes
Current main 70489061cab62786b794ac593f85933b3ade2063 still has SHA-1 at the two target call sites. Before any future merge lane, rerun Codex /review and address any findings plus the previously failing checks-node-core-runtime-infra-network-platform shard if it still reproduces on the repaired branch.
validation_commands: node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/agent-bundle-mcp-runtime.test.ts -t "recreates the session runtime when MCP config changes"
credit_notes: Preserve YonganZhang's contribution from https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/59695 in the repaired branch history and PR body.
Keep source PR credit visible: source PR #59695 by @YonganZhang; Clownfish repair should not replace this with an uncredited new PR.
If release-note context is later added, credit @YonganZhang for the original SHA-256 fingerprinting cleanup.
